title: Reduction "By restriction it suffices to treat H=G" is unjustified in Lemma 1

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Lemma 1 (forward direction) states a condition for all H âٹ† G: [H:د‡^O(H)] آ· gconn(E)(H) â‰¥ n. The proof opens with "By restriction it suffices to treat H = G", meaning one applies the argument to i_H^*(E) for each H. This reduction requires two unverified steps:

(1) If E âˆˆ د„^O_{â‰¥n} (the O-slice â‰¥n subcategory for G), then i_H^*(E) âˆˆ د„^{i_H^*O}_{â‰¥n} (the analogous subcategory for the group H with the restricted transfer system i_H^*O). This requires knowing that restriction i_H^* sends generators G_+ âٹ—_K N^T S^1 (for K âٹ† G, T âˆˆ O(K), |T| â‰¥ n) to objects in د„^{i_H^*O}_{â‰¥n}. By Mackey decomposition, i_H^*(G_+ âٹ—_K N^T S^1) decomposes into a wedge of H-spectra of the form H_+ âٹ—_{Hâˆ©gKg^{-1}} N^{g^*T} S^1, and it is not immediate that all these pieces belong to د„^{i_H^*O}_{â‰¥n}.

(2) The characteristic function satisfies د‡^{i_H^*O}(H) = د‡^O(H). This must be verified: the restriction of the transfer system to H gives د‡ of H as the same subgroup as computed from the ambient transfer system.

Neither step is carried out. The reduction is plausible but cannot be treated as obvious.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
I worked on the gap and resolved it. Summary of what I found and did.

**State on arrival.** The proof of the forward direction (Lemma `lem:forward`) already invoked Lemma `lem:restr` ("the data restricts") to justify the reduction to H=G, and that lemma's parts (1) and (3) are exactly the two facts the critic asked for. So the reduction was *cited*, but the proof of `lem:restr`(3) was compressed in precisely the place the critic flagged: it asserted that each Mackey-decomposition piece's index set "is admissible for O|_H by axioms (2),(3)" without carrying out the group-theoretic argument. I made both steps fully rigorous.

**Step (2) of the critic â€” د‡^{O|_H}(H) = د‡^O(H).** Handled by `lem:restr`(1). I rewrote its proof to be explicit: for Kâٹ†H, axiom (1) forces every J with Jâ†’K to satisfy Jâٹ†Kâٹ†H, so the defining set {J | Jâ†’K} consists of subgroups of H and is literally the same set whether the relation is read in O or in O|_H (by definition of the restricted system). Hence the intersection-minima agree; taking K=H gives د‡^{O|_H}(H)=د‡^O(H). Verified.

**Step (1) of the critic â€” i_H^*(E) âˆˆ د„^{O|_H}_{â‰¥n}.** Handled by `lem:restr`(3), which I rewrote in full. For a generator G_+ âٹ—_L N^S S^1 with S = âٹ”_i L/J_i, J_iâ†’L, |S|â‰¥n, the Mackey double-coset formula gives
  i_H^*(G_+ âٹ—_L N^S S^1) â‰ƒ â‹پ_{[g]âˆˆH\G/L} H_+ âٹ—_{M_g} N^{S_g} S^1,
with M_g = Hâˆ©gLg^{-1}, L_g = g^{-1}Hgâˆ©L, and S_g = c_g i_{L_g}^*(S) (using (eq:resnorm) plus c_g i^* S^1 â‰ƒ S^1). I then verified each summand is a defining generator of د„^{O|_H}_{â‰¥n}:
 â€¢ Cardinality: restriction and conjugation preserve |آ·|, so |S_g| = |S| â‰¥ n. (This confirms the critic's "|g^*T|=|T|".)
 â€¢ Admissibility: for a single orbit L/J (Jâ†’L), restricting to L_g gives orbits L_g/(L_gâˆ©aJa^{-1}); from Jâ†’L, axiom (2) gives aJa^{-1}â†’L and axiom (3) (subgroup L_gâٹ†L) gives L_gâˆ©aJa^{-1}â†’L_g; conjugating by g and using axiom (2) gives g(L_gâˆ©aJa^{-1})g^{-1}â†’M_g, a relation among subgroups of H, i.e. of O|_H. Hence S_g âˆˆ O|_H(M_g).
So H_+ âٹ—_{M_g} N^{S_g} S^1 is a generator of د„^{O|_H}_{â‰¥n}, and the wedge lies in the localizing subcategory. I checked this argument symbolically/group-theoretically before writing it.

I also rewrote `lem:restr`(4) for symmetry, and added Remark `rem:reduction` that explicitly states how (1)+(2)+(3) make the inequality for E at subgroup H of G *identical* to the top-group inequality for i_H^*E over (H,O|_H). The forward-direction proof now cites this remark. References and environments check out; no LaTeX toolchain is installed here so I could not compile, but I verified all \ref resolve and all environments balance.

Considering the gap fully closed.
